seoTitle: Frederickton vs Freeburn Island — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Frederickton and Freeburn Island. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Frederickton vs Freeburn Island: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Frederickton and Freeburn Island.
heading: Frederickton vs Freeburn Island — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Frederickton, New South Wales and Freeburn Island, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Freeburn Island has a lower overall climate risk score (29/100) compared to Frederickton (47/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Frederickton or Freeburn Island safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Frederickton has a flood risk score of 58/100, while Freeburn Island scores 37/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Frederickton scores 38/100 for bushfire risk. Freeburn Island scores 30/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Frederickton or Freeburn Island?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Freeburn Island has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.55% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
